name: Hiking from Hita — circular through Valle del Badiel and Monte de Utande
name_original: Utande desde Hita — circular por Valle del Badiel y Monte de Utande
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hita navigate a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural plains. The region features varied terrain, including areas like the Valle del Badiel and Monte de Utande, which offer diverse cycling experiences. Routes often follow river courses such as the Henares, providing a mix of gradients and scenic views. The area's topography supports both challenging climbs and more moderate paths suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Hita The most popular touring cycling route is Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares, a 47.1 miles (75.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Henares river, offering a circular journey through the landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop, a difficult 34.2 miles (55.1 km) path. This loop route explores historical areas and varied terrain around Brihuega. Local touring cyclists also love the Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail leading through historical towns and past the Castillo del Cid, often completed in about 6 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Hita is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and historical sites, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Hita are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hita's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Hita?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Hita, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hita?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For example, you can find options that navigate the rolling hills and river valleys at a more relaxed pace.

Q: What kind of challenges can I expect on touring cycling routes in Hita?
A: Hita's touring cycling routes are predominantly difficult, with 19 out of 25 routes rated as such. They often feature significant elevation changes, such as the 1113 meters of ascent on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185622376/trijueque-and-hita-from-torija-circular-by-jadraque-and-the-castillo-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid&lt;/a&gt; route, and cover long distances, with some exceeding 70 km.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Hita?
A: Yes, many routes in Hita are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189748619/guadalajara-from-torija-circular-by-alarilla-and-the-river-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185572381/alvar-fanez-route-and-brihuega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in Hita?
A: Routes vary significantly, but many are long-distance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189748619/guadalajara-from-torija-circular-by-alarilla-and-the-river-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares&lt;/a&gt; is 75.9 km and takes over 5 hours, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185622376/trijueque-and-hita-from-torija-circular-by-jadraque-and-the-castillo-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid&lt;/a&gt; is 73.1 km and can take over 6 hours.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Hita?
A: Hita experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter chill. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Hita?
A: The routes often pass through historical towns and natural landscapes. You can explore the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita&lt;/a&gt; itself, or cycle past impressive structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5854808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torija&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer scenic views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cogolludo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small towns and villages, offering opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or shops for refreshments. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify potential stops along the way.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Hita?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the blend of historical sites with natural beauty, and the challenging yet rewarding routes.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Hita?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Hita start from towns like Torija or Carrascosa de Henares, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check specific starting points for parking options when planning your trip.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while touring cycling in Hita?
A: The region's landscape, characterized by river valleys and agricultural plains, can offer opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially birds. Cycling through quieter, less developed areas increases your chances of encountering local fauna.
tips: This hilltop fortress can be seen from miles away. The Castillo de Hita sits on a steep cone-shaped geological hill that rises from the Alcarria.
Head up the CM-1000 road from the valley for great views of Utande Mountain and the town below.

Hita preserves its medieval old town, which was declared a historical site in 1965. Under Muslim rule, a fortress was established on top of the hill. The castle, the narrow streets and its walls are still the product of an ancient history of this pretty town! One of the most beautiful in the province!

waypoints.name: View of Utande and Monte Utande
